primary_concern: Sepsis risk with concurrent acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) and thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. AKI Stage 3 (creatinine 8.9 mg/dL, excluded as outlier) with prior Stage 1 suggests rapid renal decompensation. Thrombocytopenia (<100 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulation scoring criteria. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) and anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL) suggest possible hepatic involvement.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and thrombocytopenia meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine elevation to Stage 3 (excluded outlier) with prior Stage 1 meets KDIGO criteria for AKI
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline in 7d

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation scoring threshold
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but no evidence of vasopressor-dependent hypotension
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent MAP ≥65 mmHg + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infectious workup
Start fluid resuscitation and reassess for vasopressor support
Monitor platelet trends and consider transfusion if <50 K/uL
doctor_handoff: Review excluded creatinine outlier (8.9 mg/dL) for specimen validity. Confirm source of infection and reassess for septic shock criteria. Monitor urine output and renal function trends.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
